name: Microsoft Power Automate
description: A workflow automation tool for connecting approvals, forms, email triggers, alerts, and data movement across the systems property teams already use.
whyUseIt: Property managers spend too much time chasing repetitive admin: report assembly, reminder emails, approval routing, incident notifications, and spreadsheet updates. Power Automate helps remove low-value repetition so the team can spend more time on judgment and stakeholder work.
aiCapabilities: AI can classify incoming requests, generate draft responses, extract data from documents, and suggest next-step automations from repeated manual patterns.
workflowStage: Approvals, alerts, handoffs, and repetitive admin automation
propertyManagerWorkflow: A property manager can automate lease-date reminders, monthly report requests, contractor follow-up prompts, or tenant-service escalations without custom software development.
managerExpects: Automations that are reliable, documented, and genuinely useful rather than fragile workflows that create more confusion than they remove.
aiWorkflowImpact: AI lets teams move from rigid rule-based automations to smarter intake, routing, and summarization, especially for messy email-heavy workflows.
demoDescription: Design a flow that alerts the right people when a high-priority incident is logged and then creates a follow-up task for the next owner update.

vocabulary.definition: The event that starts an automation, such as a new form, email, or status change.
A sequence of automated steps that route data, approvals, or notifications.
The integration point that lets one system exchange information with another.

beginnerMistakes: Automating a bad process before cleaning it up
Creating flows with no clear error handling or ownership
Sending too many alerts and training the team to ignore them
